campus: Milan (University of Milan Citta Studi campus and Politecnico di Milano Leonardo Campus)
admission_type: restricted access (Limited enrolment with admission test)
academic_requirements: Applicants must hold, or obtain by 31 October 2026, a bachelor's/first-cycle degree in one of two background routes. Route 1: Biotechnology (L-2), Biology (L-13), Agriculture and Food Sciences (L-26), or Pharmacological Sciences (L-29), with at least 30 ECTS/CFU in biological areas and at least 18 ECTS/CFU across Genetics (BIO/18), Molecular Biology (BIO/11), and Biochemistry (BIO/10), with at least 6 ECTS in each of those three subjects. Route 2: Information Engineering (L-8), Computer Science (L-31), Physics (L-30), Mathematics (L-35), or Statistics (L-41), with at least 30 ECTS/CFU in computer science, information engineering, biomedical engineering, mathematics and/or statistics, including at least 6 ECTS in mathematics and at least 12 ECTS in INF/01, ING-INF/05, ING-INF/06, and/or SECS-S/01. Equivalent foreign degrees are accepted if the Teachers Council can identify the disciplines and credits; otherwise the candidate's academic records are examined in detail. Admission also requires adequate background knowledge in either genetics/molecular biology/biochemistry or computer science/information engineering/mathematics.
language_requirements: English proficiency at B2 CEFR or higher is required. The admission call asks applicants to upload a B2 English certificate if they have one, while the programme page and manifesto state that in exceptional cases candidates without a valid certificate may still be admitted if the admission interview clearly demonstrates good English proficiency. Knowledge of Italian is not required for attendance or graduation. For the optional 3-credit activity 'Additional Language Skills: Italian', students without an Italian high school diploma or degree can satisfy the requirement with an Italian certificate at A2 level or higher, issued within the previous 3 years and uploaded through http://studente.unimi.it/uploadCertificazioniLingue, or by taking the one-time SLAM entry-level test; students below A2 must attend one or more 60-hour SLAM Italian courses and pass the final test.
application_deadline_eu: Admission application: 24 March 2026 to 26 June 2026, 2:00 p.m. Interview: 1 July 2026, 9:30 a.m. Ranking publication: 7 July 2026 after 2:00 p.m. Enrolment for admitted candidates: 7 July 2026, 2:00 p.m. to 16 July 2026, 2:00 p.m.
application_deadline_non_eu: For non-EU candidates applying for a visa: admission application 24 March 2026 to 29 May 2026, 2:00 p.m.; no interview if resident abroad; ranking publication 7 July 2026 after 2:00 p.m.; Universitaly pre-enrolment deadline 31 July 2026.
required_documents: Diploma Supplement or official certificate(s)/transcript showing the awarded degree or current enrolment, passed exams, grades, and credits; accepted languages are Italian, English, French, Spanish, or German.
Passport personal-data pages or another valid identity document.
For applicants with qualifications obtained abroad: Bachelor's degree (B.Sc. or equivalent) or degree-awarding certificate.

For candidates who have not yet graduated: total credits obtained, average grade, exams taken and not yet recorded with their credits, exams not yet taken with their credits, and expected graduation date entered in the application.
Application receipt; the call states this is the only accepted proof of registration for the admission exam, and the payment receipt may also be requested.
To finalize enrolment with a foreign qualification: legalized or apostilled Bachelor's degree (or equivalent) and high school diploma.
Official Italian translations of academic qualifications, unless the original documents are in English, French, German, or Spanish.

Statement of Comparability from an ENIC-NARIC centre such as CIMEA, or a Dichiarazione di Valore; for Lisbon Recognition Convention countries, the Diploma Supplement or ARDI Statement of Correspondence may replace the DOV/Comparability statement as specified by UniMi.
For non-EU citizens: receipt of the residence-permit application or a valid residence permit.
For non-EU students residing abroad: Universitaly pre-enrolment receipt, study visa, request for residence permit, and any certificates of competence in Italian.
entry_exam_or_test: Admission is by selection interview plus CV/academic-record evaluation. Eligible applicants are scored on a 100-point scale: up to 50 points for the applicant's resume/academic record and up to 50 points for the interview. The minimum score for admission is 60/100. For A.Y. 2026/2027, interviews for Italian and EU students are held online on 1 July 2026 at 9:30 a.m.; foreign non-EU students residing in Italy must also attend. Candidates must show a valid ID at the beginning of the interview. Non-EU candidates residing abroad and applying for a visa do not take the interview and are evaluated only on their previous first-cycle study programme/resume, up to 100/100. Ranking lists for EU and non-EU candidates are published together, but visa applicants compete within the reserved quota of 10 places.

source_quotes.quote: A.Y. 2026/2027. Master programme. LM-8 R - Biotecnologie industriali. Access: Limited enrolment with admission test. Course location: Milan. Language: English.
Lecture rooms and laboratories are located: in the "Citta Studi" campus ... in the "Leonardo" Campus of the Politecnico di Milano.
Application from 24 March to 26 June 2026, 2:00 p.m. Application for NON EU visa applicants from 24 March to 29 May 2026, 2:00 p.m.
Students must be proficient in English, with a B2 or higher level of competence.
Curriculum vitae (in English) ... Diploma Supplement ... Certificate for B2-level or higher in English ... Passport.
The interview will take place exclusively online on 1 July 2026, 9.30 a.m.
Non-EU candidates residing abroad (visa applicants) are not required to attend the admission interview.
submit an application for pre-enrolment through Universitaly platform by 31 July 2026
Knowledge of Italian is not required for attendance, nor for graduation.
students without a valid language certificate may be accepted on condition that their level of English proficiency, assessed during the interview, is evidently good.
The prerequisite to access the BCG Master degree program is an adequate knowledge of the fundaments of either (1) genetics ... or (2) computer science ...

Certificates must have been earned for no more than 3 years ... For Master's degree courses requiring the English entry test: language certificates must be uploaded when submitting the admission request.
In order to enrol on a Master's degree, visa applicants must apply within 30 April 2026.
Pre-application ... must be presented on the Universitaly website within 31 July 2026, under penalty of exclusion.

uncertainty_notes: No core programme field is missing after checking the official programme page, the A.Y. 2026/27 programme-specific admission call PDF, and the A.Y. 2026/27 manifesto PDF.

There is one official-source discrepancy for non-EU visa timing: the central UniMi international-enrolment page says visa applicants to Master's degrees must apply by 30 April 2026, while the BCG programme page and the programme-specific admission call state 29 May 2026 at 2:00 p.m. for the BCG admission application and 31 July 2026 for Universitaly pre-enrolment. The programme-specific call was prioritized.

The BCG call requires a B2 English certificate only 'if in possess' and allows interview-based assessment in exceptional cases; it does not itself list the accepted certificate families or validity period. The generic UniMi language-certificates page was used only to supply institution-wide upload timing and certificate-validity rules.
